Chemical burns from sulfuric acid result in coagulative necrosis and blistering without typical singeing of hair, distinguishing them from thermal burns. Singe marks are specific to thermal injuries; thus, stating an absence of singeing reflects accurate pathophysiology and should remain correct within standard medical guidelines.
